title: Application for Refugee Sponsorship: Joint Assistance Sponsorship - Canada.ca
content:
## Application for Refugee Sponsorship: Joint Assistance Sponsorship
Get help to open an application form or to fill one out in the Help Centre .
This application includes the following items:
- Document Checklist — Joint Assistance Sponsorship [IMM 5495] ( PDF , 0.09 MB )
- Request for a Joint Assistance Sponsorship Refugee Profile [IMM 5504] ( PDF , 0.605 MB )
- Undertaking/Application for a Joint Assistance Sponsorship [IMM 1324] ( PDF , 2.37 MB )
- Sponsor Assessment [IMM 5492] ( PDF , 1.23 MB )
- Settlement Plan — Joint Assistance Sponsorship [IMM 5494] ( PDF , 0.65 MB )
- Use of a Representative [IMM 5476] ( PDF , 1.94 MB )
- Instruction Guide [IMM 5493]
This application is for Sponsorship Agreement Holders (SAH) and their Constituent Groups who wish to sponsor a refugee(s) under the Joint Assistance Sponsorship (JAS) Program.
JAS refers to a joint undertaking by the sponsoring group and Citizenship and Immigration Canada to sponsor a refugee(s) requiring special assistance and whose admissibility depends upon the additional support of a sponsor.
Refugees sponsored under the JAS program are identified as having special needs that will likely result in a longer or more difficult period of integration.
These special needs include:
- a large number of family members;
- trauma resulting from violence or torture;
- medical disabilities; and
- the effects of systemic discrimination.
Note: Sponsors living in the province of Quebec should contact the Ministère de l’Immigration, de la Francisation et de l’Intégration .
- Read your instruction guide and download your application forms.
- Complete, sign and date your application forms.
- Mail your completed application to the Resettlement Operations Centre in Ottawa (ROC-O) .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
